subject: Math
skill: Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20

lesson.story_mission: Help the Fact Family Festival light up quick mental-math paths.
lesson.mini_lesson: Build fluency within 20 by using make-10, doubles, near doubles, related facts, and missing-part strategies.
lesson.objective: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies.
lesson.concept_explanation: Fluent math uses known facts to solve quickly and accurately without counting every object.
lesson.common_misconception: A child may count all instead of using a strategy or may mix up the whole and the missing part.
lesson.real_world_connection: Facts within 20 help with games, sharing snacks, and quick classroom math.
lesson.lesson_summary: Choose a strategy, solve the fact, and use a related fact to check.
hints: Look for a friendly fact such as make 10, doubles, or a related fact.
Use the known fact to solve the unknown number.
Check that the parts and whole make sense.

hint_ladder: Look for a friendly fact such as make 10, doubles, or a related fact.
Use the known fact to solve the unknown number.
Check that the parts and whole make sense.

misconception_bank.make_ten_confusion: Learner may split numbers incorrectly when making 10.
misconception_bank.doubles_confusion: Learner may mix up doubles and near doubles.
misconception_bank.related_fact_confusion: Learner may not connect addition and subtraction facts.
misconception_bank.missing_part_confusion: Learner may confuse the whole and a missing part.

level_banks.label: Level 1: Make 10
level_banks.focus: Use ten frames to make a ten first.

hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_LVL1_Q1, notice that 8 needs 2 more to make 10.
Strategy: Split 6 into 2 and 4; then add 8 + 2 = 10 and 10 + 4 = 14.
Verify: Use subtraction to check the sum: 14 - 6 = 8.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Move 2 from 6 to 8 to make 10: 8 + 2 = 10, with 4 left. Then 10 + 4 = 14. Why it works: splitting an addend does not change the total. Check: 14 - 6 = 8.

question_audio.text: Make 10: 8 plus 6 equals the unknown The ten frame shows 8 and 6 joining to make 14.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The ten frame shows 8 and 6 joining to make 14.
questions.accessible_description: The ten frame shows 8 and 6 joining to make 14.

hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_LVL1_Q2, notice that 9 needs 1 more to make 10.
Strategy: Split 4 into 1 and 3; then add 9 + 1 = 10 and 10 + 3 = 13.
Verify: Use subtraction to check the sum: 13 - 4 = 9.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Move 1 from 4 to 9 to make 10: 9 + 1 = 10, with 3 left. Then 10 + 3 = 13. Why it works: splitting an addend does not change the total. Check: 13 - 4 = 9.

question_audio.text: Make 10: 9 plus 4 equals the unknown The ten frame shows 9 and 4 joining to make 13.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The ten frame shows 9 and 4 joining to make 13.
questions.accessible_description: The ten frame shows 9 and 4 joining to make 13.

Strategy: Split 5 into 3 and 2; then add 7 + 3 = 10 and 10 + 2 = 12.
Verify: Use subtraction to check the sum: 12 - 5 = 7.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Move 3 from 5 to 7 to make 10: 7 + 3 = 10, with 2 left. Then 10 + 2 = 12. Why it works: splitting an addend does not change the total. Check: 12 - 5 = 7.

level_banks.label: Level 2: Doubles and Near Doubles
level_banks.focus: Use doubles facts and one-more facts.

hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_LVL2_Q1, compare 6 and 6; the addends match.
Strategy: Double 6: two equal groups of 6 make 12.
Verify: Check the addition with subtraction: 12 - 6 = 6.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Two equal groups give 6 + 6 = 12. Why it works: doubling counts the same amount twice. Check: 12 - 6 = 6.

question_audio.text: Double: 6 plus 6 equals the unknown The addition model shows 6 and 6 joining to make 12.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The addition model shows 6 and 6 joining to make 12.
questions.accessible_description: The addition model shows 6 and 6 joining to make 12.

hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_LVL2_Q2, compare 8 and 8; the addends match.
Strategy: Double 8: two equal groups of 8 make 16.
Verify: Check the addition with subtraction: 16 - 8 = 8.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Two equal groups give 8 + 8 = 16. Why it works: doubling counts the same amount twice. Check: 16 - 8 = 8.

question_audio.text: Double: 8 plus 8 equals the unknown The ten frame shows 8 and 8 joining to make 16.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The ten frame shows 8 and 8 joining to make 16.
questions.accessible_description: The ten frame shows 8 and 8 joining to make 16.

hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_LVL2_Q3, compare 7 and 8; they are 1 apart.
Strategy: Use 7 + 7 = 14, then add 1 to get 15.
Verify: Check the addition with subtraction: 15 - 8 = 7.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Start with the known double 7 + 7 = 14. One addend is 1 greater, so add 1: 14 + 1 = 15. Why it works: the near-double has exactly one more than the double. Check: 15 - 8 = 7.

question_audio.text: Near double: 7 plus 8 equals the unknown A number bond shows whole 15 connected to parts 7 and 8; the unknown is 15.
question_audio.label: Read Question

level_banks.label: Level 3: Related Facts
level_banks.focus: Use fact families to connect addition and subtraction.

hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_LVL3_Q1, identify the whole 12 and the parts 5 and 7.
Strategy: Use the related addition fact 7 + 5 = 12 to find the missing part.
Verify: Add the difference and removed part: 7 + 5 = 12.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: The whole 12 is made from parts 5 and 7, so 12 - 5 = 7. Why it works: subtraction finds a missing part. Check with inverse addition: 7 + 5 = 12.

question_audio.text: Related fact: 12 minus 5 equals the unknown The subtraction model starts with 12, removes 5, and shows 7 remaining.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The subtraction model starts with 12, removes 5, and shows 7 remaining.
questions.accessible_description: The subtraction model starts with 12, removes 5, and shows 7 remaining.

hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_LVL3_Q2, identify the whole 15 and the parts 6 and 15.
Strategy: Join the parts: 9 + 6 = 15.
Verify: Undo the addition: 15 - 6 = 9.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Join 9 and 6 to make the whole 15. Why it works: addition joins two parts. Check with the inverse operation: 15 - 6 = 9.

question_audio.text: Related fact: 9 plus 6 equals the unknown The addition model shows 9 and 6 joining to make 15.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The addition model shows 9 and 6 joining to make 15.
questions.accessible_description: The addition model shows 9 and 6 joining to make 15.

hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_LVL3_Q3, identify the whole 15 and the parts 9 and 6.

Verify: Add the difference and removed part: 6 + 9 = 15.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: The whole 15 is made from parts 9 and 6, so 15 - 9 = 6. Why it works: subtraction finds a missing part. Check with inverse addition: 6 + 9 = 15.

question_audio.text: Fact family: 15 minus 9 equals the unknown A number bond shows starting whole 15, quantity 9 removed, and 6 remaining.
question_audio.label: Read Question

level_banks.label: Mixed
level_banks.focus: Solve addition and subtraction facts within 20 using any mental strategy.

questions.prompt: Mina has 3 red counters and 8 blue counters. How many counters does she have altogether?
hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_MIXED_Q1, notice that 3 needs 7 more to make 10.
Strategy: Split 8 into 7 and 1; then add 3 + 7 = 10 and 10 + 1 = 11.
Verify: Use subtraction to check the sum: 11 - 8 = 3.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Move 7 from 8 to 3 to make 10: 3 + 7 = 10, with 1 left. Then 10 + 1 = 11. Why it works: splitting an addend does not change the total. Check: 11 - 8 = 3.

question_audio.text: Mina has 3 red counters and 8 blue counters. How many counters does she have altogetherthe unknown The ten frame shows 3 and 8 joining to make 11.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The ten frame shows 3 and 8 joining to make 11.
questions.accessible_description: The ten frame shows 3 and 8 joining to make 11.

questions.prompt: A basket holds 4 apples. Sam adds 7 more apples. How many apples are in the basket now?
hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_MIXED_Q2, notice that 3 needs 7 more to make 10.
Strategy: Split 7 into 7 and 0; then add 3 + 7 = 10 and 10 + 0 = 10.
Verify: Use subtraction to check the sum: 10 - 7 = 3.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Move 7 from 7 to 3 to make 10: 3 + 7 = 10, with 0 left. Then 10 + 0 = 10. Why it works: splitting an addend does not change the total. Check: 10 - 7 = 3.

question_audio.text: A basket holds 4 apples. Sam adds 7 more apples. How many apples are in the basket nowthe unknown The addition model shows 3 and 7 joining to make 10.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The addition model shows 3 and 7 joining to make 10.
questions.accessible_description: The addition model shows 3 and 7 joining to make 10.

questions.prompt: There are 2 birds on a fence and 9 birds join them. How many birds are on the fence?
hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_MIXED_Q3, notice that 2 needs 8 more to make 10.
Strategy: Split 8 into 8 and 0; then add 2 + 8 = 10 and 10 + 0 = 10.
Verify: Use subtraction to check the sum: 10 - 8 = 2.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Move 8 from 8 to 2 to make 10: 2 + 8 = 10, with 0 left. Then 10 + 0 = 10. Why it works: splitting an addend does not change the total. Check: 10 - 8 = 2.

question_audio.text: There are 2 birds on a fence and 9 birds join them. How many birds are on the fencethe unknown A number bond shows whole 10 connected to parts 2 and 8; the unknown is 10.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: A number bond shows whole 10 connected to parts 2 and 8; the unknown is 10.
questions.accessible_description: A number bond shows whole 10 connected to parts 2 and 8; the unknown is 10.

questions.prompt: Two teams each collect 4 beanbags. How many beanbags do they collect altogether?
hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_MIXED_Q4, compare 4 and 4; the addends match.
Strategy: Double 4: two equal groups of 4 make 8.
Verify: Check the addition with subtraction: 8 - 4 = 4.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Two equal groups give 4 + 4 = 8. Why it works: doubling counts the same amount twice. Check: 8 - 4 = 4.

question_audio.text: Two teams each collect 4 beanbags. How many beanbags do they collect altogetherthe unknown The addition model shows 4 and 4 joining to make 8.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The addition model shows 4 and 4 joining to make 8.
questions.accessible_description: The addition model shows 4 and 4 joining to make 8.

questions.prompt: One shelf has 5 books and another has 6 books. How many books are on both shelves?
hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_MIXED_Q5, compare 3 and 4; they are 1 apart.
Strategy: Use 3 + 3 = 6, then add 1 to get 7.
Verify: Check the addition with subtraction: 7 - 4 = 3.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: Start with the known double 3 + 3 = 6. One addend is 1 greater, so add 1: 6 + 1 = 7. Why it works: the near-double has exactly one more than the double. Check: 7 - 4 = 3.

question_audio.text: One shelf has 5 books and another has 6 books. How many books are on both shelvesthe unknown The ten frame shows 3 and 4 joining to make 7.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The ten frame shows 3 and 4 joining to make 7.
questions.accessible_description: The ten frame shows 3 and 4 joining to make 7.

questions.prompt: Kai has 20 stickers and gives 7 away. How many stickers remain?
hints: Focus: In G2M_OP_003_MIXED_Q6, identify the whole 20 and the parts 7 and 13.
Strategy: Use the related addition fact 13 + 7 = 20 to find the missing part.
Verify: Add the difference and removed part: 13 + 7 = 20.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: The whole 20 is made from parts 7 and 13, so 20 - 7 = 13. Why it works: subtraction finds a missing part. Check with inverse addition: 13 + 7 = 20.

question_audio.text: Kai has 20 stickers and gives 7 away. How many stickers remainthe unknown The subtraction model starts with 20, removes 7, and shows 13 remaining.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: The subtraction model starts with 20, removes 7, and shows 13 remaining.
questions.accessible_description: The subtraction model starts with 20, removes 7, and shows 13 remaining.

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting a math mission about Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20. Look at the story and think about what the characters need to solve. Math missions are easier when you name the numbers and the model first. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20. Look at the ten frame and connect each part to the math words. Use the vocabulary on the page to explain your thinking. Remember to check place value, units, or symbols before you answer. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20. Follow the model one step at a time. Say what each number means before you look at the final answer. If a symbol appears, read it in words like plus, minus, equals, greater than, or less than. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo helps you try Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20 with support. Build or study the model first, then connect it to the question. A good strategy is to explain the answer in math words before choosing. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the visual model to help you think about Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. Use the visual model to help you think about Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Use the visual model to help you think about Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Check your strategy before you choose or write an answer. When you are ready, answer the question and keep going.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your mission work on Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes help show what to practice next. Save your growth data to continue.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Fluency With Addition and Subtraction Within 20. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, and hints used. Use the recommendation to decide what to practice next. You can continue to Skill Practice or replay the mission.
profile.label: Read This Page
